summary: Connector recovery SLA — 60-minute target after upstream outage
description_md:
As a workspace-admin, I want the Connector Health Monitor to automatically recover and resume data synchronization within 60 minutes after an upstream data source outage, so that downstream reports and compliance workflows have bounded staleness guarantees.

**Background**:

When a connected data source (Salesforce, SAP S/4) experiences an outage, connectors enter a degraded state and must retry upon restoration. Without a defined recovery SLA, tenants cannot guarantee data freshness for time-sensitive compliance reporting and audit log exports.

**Acceptance Criteria**:

1. Given an upstream data source outage, when the source becomes available again, then the connector must detect the restoration and resume full synchronization within 60 minutes.

2. Given a connector in recovery mode, when the 60-minute SLA window is approaching (50 minutes elapsed), then the Connector Health Monitor must raise a warning alert to the workspace-admin.

3. Given a connector that fails to recover within 60 minutes, when the SLA is breached, then the system must create an incident ticket and notify the workspace-admin via email.

4. Given a successful recovery, when synchronization completes, then the audit log must record the outage duration, recovery time, and any data gap remediation actions taken.

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Implementation complete. Recovery logic uses exponential backoff with 60-minute cap. Warning alert fires at 50 minutes. Verified with simulated Salesforce outage scenario.
